definition: Average log predictive density assigned to held-out responses
direction: Higher, or less negative, is better

definition: Exponential of the per-trial log-likelihood difference
caveat: Geometric average density ratio, not percent accuracy

definition: Twice the parameter count minus twice the in-sample log likelihood
caveat: In-sample fit penalized by 2 per free parameter

definition: Log sample size times parameter count minus twice the in-sample log likelihood
2.5th and 97.5th percentiles after resampling run sequences
direction: Excluding zero supports a stable direction

definition: Fraction of bootstrap differences at or below zero
direction: Smaller means fewer resamples reverse the effect
caveat: Directional bootstrap tail proportion, not a standalone classical p-value

definition: Diagonal entry of the fitted transition matrix
direction: Closer to 1 means greater state persistence
caveat: Compare with the chain's stationary probability

definition: Reciprocal of one minus self-transition probability
direction: Expected trials in a homogeneous geometric run
caveat: A teaching translation, not the posterior MAP run statistic

definition: Fraction of responses closer to the prior center than to the current stimulus
direction: Larger means more responses lie closer to the prior
caveat: Descriptive classification, not a recovered latent state

definition: 2.5th to 97.5th percentile across complete simulated datasets
direction: Coverage indicates compatibility with simulated variability
caveat: Intervals use 100 complete simulated datasets per model

definition: PPC metrics after symmetrically excluding smoothed marginal-MAP lapse classifications
direction: Compare with the complete-model PPC rather than reading it alone
caveat: Uses the complete response sequence, is not Viterbi decoding or an online lapse detector, and does not replace the all-trial PPC
